sections.paragraph: πορφύρα, ας, ἡ [origin uncertain, loanword in Lat. purpura ‘shell-fish, purple dye, purple-dyed cloth’] in NT not of purple dye itself, but of cloth or a garment colored w. purple dye and in the high grades very costly, purple garment Mk 15:17, 20; Lk 16:19; perh. of yard goods rather than finished garment purple (cloth) Rv 18:12.
